purpose: Teach an agent how to interpret PanKgraph islet functional assay features for insulin and glucagon secretion data.
core_rules: Preserve feature names exactly when matching fields in data.
Interpret IEQ-normalized values as secretion normalized to islet mass.
AUC features represent secretion integrated over a stimulation period or response phase.
Basal features represent baseline secretion rates before the main stimulus.
SI means stimulation index: fold change or stimulation relative to baseline or an appropriate reference condition.
II means inhibition index: fold change or suppression under an inhibitory condition relative to baseline or an appropriate reference condition.
Do not compare insulin units and glucagon units directly; insulin features use ng/100 IEQs or ng/100 IEQs/min, while glucagon features use pg/100 IEQs or pg/100 IEQs/min.
High glucose is 16.7 mM glucose. Low glucose is 1.7 mM glucose. Basal glucose is 5.6 mM glucose.
IBMX 100 is annotated as an increased intracellular cAMP condition. It is usually associated with increased insulin secretion and increased glucagon secretion.
Ad 1 refers to adrenaline/epinephrine at 1 µM. In the source figure, this condition is associated with reduced insulin secretion and increased glucagon secretion.

KCI 20 indicates potassium chloride at 20 mM. Biologically, it depolarizes endocrine cells and can trigger Ca2+-dependent secretion of islet hormones, including insulin and glucagon, depending on the measured cell/output.
When reporting interpretation, state hormone, condition, measurement type, normalization, and expected biological meaning.

global_terms.IEQ: Islet equivalent; used here as normalization to islet mass.
global_terms.AUC: Area under the curve; integrated secretion over a defined time window or stimulation period.
global_terms.SI: Stimulation index; fold stimulation induced by treatment relative to baseline or reference.
global_terms.II: Inhibition index; fold inhibition or suppression induced by treatment relative to baseline or reference.
global_terms.KCI: Source label for potassium chloride depolarization condition; match as written in data, interpret biologically as KCI.
global_terms.phase: first-phase and second-phase are specfic for first-phase AUC and second-phase AUC features for AUC during high glucose stimulation. So do not call anthing else as phase 1/2/3.. to avoid confusion with specfic term.

feature_dictionary.interpretation: Baseline insulin secretion at normal glucose before stimulated conditions.
Immediate first-peak insulin release during early high-glucose stimulation.
Second-phase insulin release during sustained high-glucose stimulation.
Total insulin secretion above baseline during high-glucose stimulation.
Fold stimulation of insulin secretion at high glucose, usually peak or stimulated response relative to baseline.
Insulin secretion during high glucose plus IBMX, reflecting cAMP-amplified insulin secretory capacity.
Fold stimulation of insulin secretion during high glucose plus IBMX relative to baseline.
Insulin secretion during low glucose plus adrenaline/epinephrine, an insulin-inhibitory condition.

feature_dictionary.normalization: relative to baseline or reference condition
feature_dictionary.interpretation: Fold inhibition of insulin secretion under low glucose plus adrenaline/epinephrine.
Insulin secretion after potassium chloride depolarization, bypassing glucose sensing/metabolism and directly testing Ca2+-dependent secretion.
Fold stimulation of insulin secretion after potassium chloride depolarization relative to baseline.
Baseline glucagon secretion at basal glucose before stimulated or inhibitory conditions.
Glucagon response during high glucose, commonly interpreted as suppression relative to baseline.

feature_dictionary.normalization: relative to baseline or reference condition
feature_dictionary.interpretation: Fold inhibition or suppression of glucagon during high-glucose exposure.
Glucagon secretion during high glucose plus IBMX, reflecting cAMP-amplified secretory response.
Fold stimulation of glucagon secretion under high glucose plus IBMX.
Glucagon secretion during low glucose plus adrenaline/epinephrine, a counterregulatory condition.
Fold stimulation of glucagon secretion during low glucose plus adrenaline/epinephrine.
Glucagon secretion after potassium chloride depolarization, bypassing glucose sensing/metabolism and directly testing Ca2+-dependent secretion.
Fold stimulation of glucagon secretion after potassium chloride depolarization relative to baseline.

response_template.cohort_comparison: prohibit direct comparison of insulin and glucagon features due to different units and scales; interpret each hormone's features within their own context.
response_template.missing_feature: This trait is not in the PanKgraph functional feature dictionary. Report it as unmatched and avoid biological interpretation unless additional metadata is provided.

examples.agent_interpretation: INS-G 16.7 SI measures insulin stimulation index during 16.7 mM high-glucose exposure. It is a fold-change metric relative to baseline; higher values generally suggest stronger glucose-stimulated insulin secretion.

GCG-G 16.7 II measures inhibition of glucagon secretion during 16.7 mM high-glucose exposure. It should be treated as a fold inhibition/suppression metric, with exact direction confirmed from the dataset's index definition.
INS-KCI 20 AUC measures IEQ-normalized insulin secretion after potassium chloride depolarization. This bypasses glucose sensing/metabolism and reflects downstream Ca2+-dependent secretory capacity.
